user_query: Read input/paper.txt and input/config.json. Do a preflight inspection first: compute page_count by counting lines that start with "=== Page", char_count (total characters), word_count (whitespace-delimited words), and a rough token_estimate as round(word_count * 1.33). Write these to output/preflight.json. Then build a semantic highlight plan from the paper text following stable categories: goal, motivation, method, contribution, result (required); definitions and limitations (optional, include only if config.include_optional is true and not listed in config.disabled_optional). Respect config.max_per_page, config.max_total, and config.min_score. For each selected highlight, include page_index (0-based), section (if you can infer from headings), text (the sentence), category (one of the allowed categories), score (float), and reasons (list of short labels). Write the plan to output/highlight_plan.json and include a metadata block that mirrors the config (highlight_level, opacity, include_optional, disabled_optional, max_per_page, max_total, min_score) plus summary counts: total_highlights and per_page_counts. Ensure all five required categories appear at least once, every highlight’s score >= min_score, and no page exceeds max_per_page or total exceeds max_total. Finally, if config.noteMode is not "none", write output/notes.json with: metadata.note_mode resolved to its alias ("default" should resolve to "flow"), metadata.add_tldr, metadata.add_section_flow, and metadata.section_items (3 for flow/default, 4 for full, 0 for none). Include a tldr string between 30 and 120 words and a section_flows array with exactly section_items entries ({ section, summary }). Do not quote paper sentences verbatim inside notes; paraphrase. If config.noteMode is "none", write notes.json with the metadata only. Place all outputs under output/.
